channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of himself
item.description: The video also features children holding gold balloons also made to look like Trump and a renovated beachfront with luxury hotels and money flying in the air.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane at Washington National
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration says an American Airlines plane arriving at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport discontinued its landing, performing a go-around to avoid getting too close to another aircraft departing from the same runway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The US Christian population has declined for years. A new survey shows that drop leveling off
item.description: The number of Americans who identify as Christian has declined steadily for years, but that drop shows signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain convicted of murder, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas will turn over bodies of 4 Israeli hostages in exchange for release of hundreds of prisoners
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas will return the bodies of four dead Israeli hostages on Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, the group said, just days before the first phase of the ceasefire between the warring parties was to expire.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The National Archives is nonpartisan but has found itself targeted by Trump
item.description: As President Donald Trump moves to overhaul the federal government with astonishing speed, he has wreaked havoc on one agency long known for its nonpartisanship and revered for its mission: the National Archives and Records Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson And Gary Fields, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: University suspends fraternity accused of ‘inhumane’ hazing that left pledge in fear
item.description: A fraternity at Miami University in Ohio has been suspended after a student complained of "inhumane" hazing and undergoing abuse, according to a hazing incident report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis sits upright in an armchair as prayers for his recovery continue
item.description: Doctors have said he isn't out of danger, but hasn't had any further respiratory crises since Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House GOP, after pulling budget resolution, brings it back for a vote
item.description: House GOP leaders brought their budget resolution to the floor for the vote, just minutes after pulling it at the last minute Tuesday evening as they struggled to win over a handful of conservative fiscal hawks who had threatened to tank the framework for President Trump's ambitious legislative agen
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: White House to take over press pool amid 'Gulf of America' fight with AP
item.description: The White House said Tuesday that it would take over which outlets are allowed into the press pool covering the president, wrestling such control from the White House Correspondents' Association.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest Airlines plane has near-collision with jet at Midway Airport
item.description: A Southwest Airlines plane and private jet had a near-collision at Midway Airport in Chicago, Illinois on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Ponzio, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security Administration closes Office of Transformation
item.description: The Office of Transformation, according to its website, is responsible for “strategic guidance and oversight of enterprise-wide initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines flight forced to make emergency landing at Newark
item.description: A United Airlines flight carrying nearly 200 passengers made an emergency landing at Newark Liberty International Airport after a "possible mechanical issue" mid-flight, the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chris Jasper of the Isley Brothers dies at 73
item.description: Chris Jasper, a member of the Isley Brothers and Isley-Jasper-Isley, passed away on Sunday. He was 73 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What will win at a nailbiter Oscars? AP's film writers make their predictions
item.description: By the time the Oscars wind around after months of other award shows and constant handicapping, the prizes can feel almost predetermined. Not this year.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Catholic community in Utah 'united in prayer' for the health of Pope Francis
item.description: The pope showed some slight improvements in recent tests, and as he continues to recover, members of the Catholic faith around the world — including in Utah — continue to pray for his health.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump immigration policy allowing arrests in churches for some religious groups
item.description: A federal judge on Monday blocked immigration agents from conducting enforcement operations in houses of worship for Quakers and a handful of other religious groups.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer shot and killed while responding to a hospital intensive care unit where authorities said a gunman had shot three hospital employees and taken staff members hostage last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Under threat of termination, VA workers return to the office
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered federal workers to return to the office, and that’s exactly what happened at the Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday. “This is an exciting day,” VA Secretary Doug Collins said in a video posted on social media that included footage of him greeting employees.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Minimum wage workers would have to work 106 hours to afford average apartment, report finds
item.description: A new report found that a minimum-wage employee would have to work 106 hours a week to afford a median-priced apartment in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states, including Florida
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Condemned South Carolina killer chooses to be executed by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Condemned South Carolina inmate Brad Sigmon has chosen to die next month by a firing squad, a method of execution that has not been used in the U.S. in 15 years. Sigmon is scheduled to die on March 7.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bell takes only lead in OT to edge Hocevar, Larson in another thrilling NASCAR finish in Atlanta
item.description: Christopher Bell celebrated a drought-ending victory for himself and Joe Gibbs Racing after a dramatic comeback in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Key federal agencies refuse to comply with Musk's latest demand in his cost-cutting crusade
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Key U.S. agencies, including the FBI, State Department and the Pentagon, have instructed their employees not to comply with cost-cutting chief Elon Musk's latest demand that federal workers explain what they accomplished last week — or risk losing their job.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, ERIC TUCKER and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
